input.initialPrompt: Convert my simple-packages-config/LegacyApp project to Central Package Management.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to convert the `simple-packages-config/LegacyApp` project to Central Package Management (CPM). Let me first explore the project structure to understand what we're working with.

Wait, the user mentioned "simple-packages-config" which likely uses `packages.config` format. The skill description says "DO NOT USE FOR: packages.config projects (must migrate to PackageReference first)". Let me first check what format the project uses before deciding whether to use the skill.

Let me explore the project first.

data.content: This project uses the legacy `packages.config` format, which must first be migrated to `PackageReference` before CPM can be applied. I'll do both steps: convert to SDK-style with PackageReference, then set up Central Package Management.
data.reasoningText:
This project uses the old `packages.config` format with a legacy-style `.csproj` file (non-SDK style). The CPM skill explicitly says "DO NOT USE FOR: packages.config projects (must migrate to PackageReference first)".

So I need to:
1. First migrate from `packages.config` to `PackageReference` format
2. Then set up Central Package Management with `Directory.Packages.props`

Let me do this manually:

1. Convert the `.csproj` to SDK-style format with `PackageReference`
2. Create a `Directory.Packages.props` file with `<ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>`


3. Remove the `packages.config` file

The project currently references Newtonsoft.Json 13.0.3 targeting .NET Framework 4.8. I'll convert it to an SDK-style project targeting net48 as a Library, then set up the central package management file in the solution root with the Newtonsoft.Json version pinned to 13.0.3.

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-4ed3593eca87420d9b14b0224861e4f5/simple-packages-config/LegacyApp/LegacyApp.csproj", "old_str": "<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.8</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="Newtonsoft.Json, Version=13.0.0.0">
      <HintPath>..\packages\Newtonsoft.Json.13.0.3\lib\net45\Newtonsoft.Json.dll</HintPath>
    </Reference>
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>", "new_str": "<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net48</TargetFramework>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>"}

data.content:
Done! Here's what I did:

1. **Converted `LegacyApp.csproj`** from legacy format to SDK-style with `PackageReference` (no version attribute — managed centrally)
2. **Created `Directory.Packages.props`** at the `simple-packages-config/` root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and `Newtonsoft.Json` version `13.0.3`
3. **Removed `packages.config`**

The project now uses Central Package Management. Package versions are controlled in `Directory.Packages.props`, and the `.csproj` only declares which packages it needs.
